docs.title: Patchmatic
docs.text: Patchmatic is a macOS application for recording your Eurorack patches — a mix of Modulargrid and VCV Rack without the audio. The name comes from "Patch Schematic"; the original idea was a tool for creating easy-to-read schematics to enable efficient repatching.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
Module design - Place knobs, patch points, buttons, toggle switches, sliders, and screens on a panel - Adjustable control size, orientation, and label positioning - Import a panel photo — controls are detected automatically

Rack assembly - Arrange modules in cases with 1U and 3U rows at any HP width - Stack multiple cases on a free-form canvas

Patching - Draw cables between any two patch points, across cases - Physics-based cable rendering with color selection - Route cables with intermediate pin points - Add notes to patch points, modules, and patches - Group modules with color-coded labels - Multiple patches per rack

Sets - Organize patches into named, ordered sets for live performance or workflow

Export - Print or export patches to PDF

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• macOS 14.0 (Sonoma) or later
  • Apple Silicon or Intel

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Download Patchmatic-<version>.dmg from the Releases page.
  2. Open the DMG and drag Patchmatic to Applications.
  3. First launch: right-click the app and select Open (the app is not notarized).

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Patchmatic is organized into four modes, selectable from the toolbar at the top of the window: Modules, Racks, Patches, and Sets. The typical workflow moves through them in that order.

docs.title: 1. Create a module
docs.text:
Switch to Modules mode. Click the camera icon button in the library toolbar to import a photo of your hardware module. Patchmatic will offer to detect controls automatically from the image. Review the results and adjust as needed.

To create a blank module instead, use the + button.

See Module Editor for full details.

docs.title: 2. Build a rack
docs.text:
Switch to Racks mode. Create a rack, add a case, configure its row width, and add rows. Drag modules from the strip at the bottom of the canvas into rows.

See Rack Editor for full details.

docs.title: 3. Create a patch
docs.text:
Switch to Patches mode. Click the + button and select a rack to create a patch for it. Drag from one patch point to another to draw a cable.

Each patch independently stores cable connections, knob positions, notes, and other state.

See Patch Editor for full details.

docs.title: 4. Organize into sets
docs.text:
Switch to Sets mode to group patches into an ordered list. Use the arrow keys or navigation buttons to step through them.

See Sets for full details.

docs.title: Saving changes
docs.text: When you make changes in the Module or Rack editors, a Save/Discard toolbar appears at the bottom of the canvas. You must explicitly save before switching away, or you will be prompted to save or discard your changes.

docs.title: Print to PDF
docs.text:
Press Cmd+P (or File > Print Patch…) while in Patches or Sets mode to open the print dialog.

The output includes: - Patch name and rack name as a header - Patch notes (if any) - The full rack canvas with all modules rendered at their current knob values - All cables for the current patch - Patch point sequence numbers (if the Numbers toggle is on) - Control labels (always shown in print, regardless of the Labels toggle)

Page orientation is set automatically — landscape if the rack content is wider than tall, portrait otherwise. Content is scaled to fit the page with 18pt margins. Use the system print dialog to save as PDF instead of sending to a printer.

docs.title: Module Editor
docs.text: The Module Editor is where you define the layout of individual Eurorack modules. Each module definition serves as a reusable template that can be placed into racks.

docs.title: Creating a module from a photo
docs.text:
Click the camera icon button in the library toolbar, or drag an image file onto the library (supported formats: PNG, JPEG, TIFF, HEIC). You can also use Modules > New Module from Image (Cmd+N).

Patchmatic creates a new module, sets the photo as the background image, and prompts you to run automatic control detection. The ML model identifies knobs, patch points, toggle switches, buttons, and sliders and places them on the panel, and estimates the module's format (1U or 3U) and width in HP.

Review the results after detection — some controls may be missed or misclassified. Adjust, delete, or add controls as needed. To re-run detection at any time, click the Analyse Background Image button (cpu icon) in the canvas toolbar, or use Cmd+Shift+A.

To create a blank module instead, click the + button or use Cmd+Shift+N.

docs.title: Module properties
docs.text: Edit the module's name, format (3U or 1U), and width in HP in the inspector panel on the right. Changing the format rescales the HP width proportionally.

docs.title: Adding controls manually
docs.text:
Select a control type from the canvas toolbar to enter placement mode, then click on the canvas to place the control. Clicking the active button again exits placement mode. Available types:

Type Description Knob Rotary control Patch Point 3.5 mm jack Toggle Switch Multi-position switch Button Momentary or latching button Slider Linear fader Screen Rectangular display area

docs.title: Control properties
docs.text:
Select a control to edit its properties in the inspector:

  • Size — small, medium, large, or extra large (not applicable to patch points, which are always medium)
  • Orientation — vertical or horizontal (toggle switches and sliders)
  • Label — position (above, below, left, right, or off), font size, and offset
  • State Label — separate label position settings for the state indicator (buttons and toggle switches)
  • States — named states for buttons and toggle switches (e.g. "Off", "On"); minimum 2, no maximum
  • Width / Height — independent dimensions for screens
  • Length — track length for sliders

When two or more controls are selected, the inspector shows batch editing options: bulk label prefix, kind, size, and label positioning.

docs.title: Selecting and editing controls
docs.text: Action Behaviour Click control Select single control Shift+Click Add or remove from selection Drag on empty space Pan canvas Shift+Drag on empty space Marquee selection Click empty space Clear selection Click overlapping controls repeatedly Cycle through the stack Cmd+A Select all controls Cmd+D Duplicate selected controls (offset slightly) Delete Remove selected controls Arrow keys Nudge selected controls 0.5 pt Shift+Arrow keys Nudge 5 pt

docs.title: Resizing controls
docs.text:
Screen and slider controls can be resized by dragging a handle on their selection highlight:

Control Handle location Resizes Screen Bottom-right corner Width and height Horizontal slider Right edge Length Vertical slider Bottom edge Length

docs.title: Alignment and layout tools
docs.text:
With multiple controls selected, the canvas toolbar provides:

  • Snap to Grid — snaps selected controls to the nearest grid intersection
  • Centre Horizontally / Vertically — aligns to the average position across the selection
  • Distribute Horizontally / Vertically — evenly spaces controls between the two outermost positions (requires 3+ controls)

docs.title: Background image
docs.text:
When a photo is imported, it appears behind the controls on the canvas. Use the Image Opacity slider in the window toolbar to fade it while positioning controls. To replace the background image without re-running detection, click the photo icon in the canvas toolbar.

Toggle Show Detections to show or hide the color-coded detection bounding boxes from the last analysis run. Toggle Hide Controls to temporarily hide placed controls and see the background image clearly.

docs.title: Canvas navigation
docs.text:
• Scroll wheel or pinch — zoom (1×–8×)
  • Option+Drag or drag on empty space — pan
  • Zoom In / Zoom Out — toolbar buttons or Cmd+= / Cmd+-
  • Frame — reset zoom and center the view (Cmd+0)

docs.title: Saving and discarding changes
docs.text:
Any change enters edit mode. A Save/Discard toolbar appears at the bottom of the canvas. Switching to another module while in edit mode is blocked until you save or discard. Saving a module that is already placed in a rack will update all existing placements.

All edits support Undo/Redo (Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z).

docs.title: Patch Editor
docs.text: A patch is a snapshot of a rack: it stores cable connections, control positions, notes, and other state independently from every other patch on the same rack. You can have as many patches per rack as you like.

docs.title: Creating a patch
docs.text: In Patches mode, click + in the patch list. A submenu lists all racks — click a rack name to create a patch for it. The patch opens showing the rack layout.

docs.title: Drawing cables
docs.text:
Drag from a patch point to another patch point to create a cable. A preview cable is shown while dragging. The cable is created when you release over a valid target. Colors are auto-assigned from the 24-color palette in round-robin order.

To change a cable's color, right-click the cable and select Change Color, then pick from the color grid.

docs.title: Selecting and deleting cables
docs.text:
• Right-click a cable to select it and show its context menu
  • Shift+Click to add a cable to the selection
  • Right-click a patch point to select all cables connected to it
  • Delete selected cables with the Delete key or via the context menu

docs.title: Routing cables with pins
docs.text:
Pins are intermediate waypoints that redirect cable routing.

  • Cmd+Click on a cable to insert a pin at that location
  • Drag a pin to reposition it
  • Right-click a pin and select Delete Cable Pin to remove it, or select it and press Delete

Pins attached to a case move with the case when it is repositioned.

docs.title: Control interaction
docs.text:
Knobs — drag vertically to adjust (up increases, down decreases).

Sliders — drag along the slider axis.

Toggle switches and buttons — click to advance to the next state, cycling through all named states.

Screens — hover over a screen to reveal a toolbar: - + — attach an image (cropped to the control's aspect ratio) - ← / → — cycle through multiple attached images - Magnifier — zoom the current image - ✕ — delete the current image

All control values are stored per patch and restored when you switch back to a patch.

docs.title: Module notes
docs.text:
Patch point notes — right-click while hovering a patch point to add or edit a short text note. Notes appear as indicators on the module panel; hover to read them.

General module notes — right-click on the module body (not over a patch point) and select Add Note to place a free-form text note on the panel.

Both types of notes are stored per patch.

docs.title: Groups
docs.text:
Groups are named overlays that color-highlight sets of modules for organizational purposes. Click the Groups toolbar toggle to show or hide group overlays and the floating group list.

  • Create a group — right-click a module and select New Group with [name]. To include multiple modules, select them first, then right-click.
  • Add / remove modules — right-click a module and use Add to Group or Remove from Group
  • Hover a group card — highlights that group's modules and fades others
  • Click a group card — animates the canvas to fit all modules in the group
  • Right-click a group card — rename, edit notes, or delete the group

docs.title: Patch notes
docs.text:
Each patch has a free-form text field and an optional audio file attachment.

Open the patch notes sheet from the Patches menu, the canvas context menu (Patch Notes…), or by clicking the patch notes overlay on the canvas (visible when the Notes toolbar toggle is on). The overlay shows the first line of patch notes and an audio icon if an audio file is attached.

docs.title: Toolbar toggles
docs.text: Toggle Action Labels Show or hide control labels Groups Show or hide group overlays Numbers Show or hide patch point sequence numbers Notes Show or hide the patch notes overlay

docs.title: Canvas navigation
docs.text:
• Scroll wheel or pinch — zoom (0.25×–5.0×)
  • Drag on empty space — pan
  • Frame to Fit — fits all cases to the viewport (Cmd+0 or toolbar button)

docs.title: Rack Editor
docs.text: The Rack Editor is where you assemble module definitions into a physical case layout.

docs.title: Racks and cases
docs.text:
A rack is a top-level container that holds one or more cases. Cases represent physical enclosures. Multiple cases per rack let you model a multi-case setup as a single unit.

To create a rack, click + in the rack list (or Cmd+N). To add a case to a rack, click the New Case toolbar button, use Cmd+Shift+C, or right-click on empty canvas space.

docs.title: Configuring a case
docs.text:
• Rename — click the case name in its header
  • HP width — click the HP value in the case header; applies to all rows in the case
  • Add a row — click the gear icon in the case header (or right-click the case) and select Add 3U Row or Add 1U Row
  • Remove a row — same menu, then choose the row to remove; this also removes all modules in that row

New cases start with two rows by default.

docs.title: Placing modules
docs.text:
Modules are placed from the module strip at the bottom of the canvas. Drag a module thumbnail from the strip onto a row. A dashed outline shows the target position while dragging.

Placement rules: - A 3U module can only be placed in a 3U row; a 1U module only in a 1U row - The module must fit within the available HP space in the row

To remove a module, select it and press Delete, or right-click and choose Delete.

docs.title: Moving modules
docs.text:
Drag a module within its row to reposition it. Modules snap to the HP grid. Modules cannot be moved between cases by dragging — remove and re-place instead.

Select multiple modules with Shift+Click or a marquee drag, then drag one to move all selected modules together.

Use the Left/Right arrow keys to nudge selected modules by 1 HP.

docs.title: Arranging cases on the canvas
docs.text: Drag a case by its header to reposition it freely on the canvas. Guide lines appear when a case aligns with another, and cases snap to an 8HP grid while dragging. Selecting multiple cases and dragging one moves all selected cases together.

docs.title: Canvas navigation
docs.text:
• Scroll wheel or pinch — zoom (0.25×–5.0×)
  • Drag on empty space — pan
  • Frame to Fit — fits all cases to the viewport (Cmd+0 or toolbar button)

Zoom and pan position are saved between sessions.

docs.title: Saving and discarding changes
docs.text:
Any modification enters edit mode. A Save/Discard toolbar appears at the bottom of the canvas. If the rack has existing patches, saving will show a confirmation. Switching to a different rack while in edit mode triggers an alert.

All edits support Undo/Redo (Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z).

docs.title: Sets
docs.text: A set is an ordered list of patches. Sets are useful for organizing a live performance sequence or a workflow that steps through related configurations in a defined order.

docs.title: Creating a set
docs.text: In Sets mode, click + in the set list (or Cmd+N) to create a new set.

docs.title: Adding and reordering patches
docs.text:
When a set is selected, the patches it contains are shown below the set list. Click Add Patch at the bottom of that list to add patches from the document. A patch can appear in multiple sets.

Drag a patch row to reorder it within the set. To remove a patch from the set, right-click it and select Remove from Set — the patch itself is not deleted.

docs.title: Navigating a set
docs.text:
Use the Left / Right arrow keys (or Cmd+← / Cmd+→) to step through patches, or click the navigation buttons in the bar at the bottom of the canvas. The current position is shown as "N / Total — Patch Name".

Clicking a patch row in the list also jumps directly to it.

docs.title: Read-only canvas
docs.text:
The canvas in Sets mode is read-only. Cables cannot be created or deleted, controls cannot be adjusted, and no context menus appear. The patch notes overlay is still visible and can be clicked to read the notes for the current patch.

To make changes, switch to Patches mode, edit the patch there, and return to Sets mode.

docs.title: Global
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+1 Switch to Modules mode Cmd+2 Switch to Racks mode Cmd+3 Switch to Patches mode Cmd+4 Switch to Sets mode Cmd+Z Undo Cmd+Shift+Z Redo Cmd+= Zoom in Cmd+- Zoom out Cmd+0 Frame to fit Cmd+Shift+L Toggle labels Cmd+P Print / export to PDF

docs.title: Modules Mode
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+N New module from image Cmd+Shift+N New blank module Cmd+R Rename module Cmd+D Duplicate module (library) / duplicate selected controls (canvas) Delete Delete selected module(s) (library) / delete selected control(s) (canvas) Cmd+Shift+A Analyse background image Cmd+A Select all controls Arrow keys Nudge selected controls 0.5 pt Shift+Arrow keys Nudge selected controls 5 pt

docs.title: Racks Mode
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+N New rack Cmd+R Rename rack Cmd+Shift+C New case Cmd+D Duplicate rack Cmd+A Select all modules Delete Remove selected modules or cases Left / Right Arrow Nudge selected module(s) ±1 HP

docs.title: Patches Mode
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+R Rename patch Cmd+D Duplicate patch Delete Delete selected cables or pin Cmd+Click cable Insert cable pin at click location

docs.title: Sets Mode
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+N New set Cmd+R Rename set Left Arrow / Cmd+← Previous patch in set Right Arrow / Cmd+→ Next patch in set
